finding: Skill-first guidance and CLI JSON are much better than the old CLI-only flow, but the boundary is still easy to misread: agents can expect a playbook when none exists, treat long CLI JSON as manual prose instead of structured tool output, miss that `artifacts/` evidence requires `git add -f`, or lose track of which dirty files belong to the current task. `guide` can also overgeneralize small evidence backfill work into a heavy `create-atom` route.
expectedBehavior: ATM should make the skill/CLI contract explicit and low-friction: tell the agent when there is no playbook, surface the one next structured action, distinguish skill authority from CLI evidence, warn when target artifacts are ignored and need force-add, and classify dirty files as in-scope versus unrelated before mutation.
evidenceOrRepro: Repaired on 2026-06-28 by hardening prompt-scoped `next` summaries with explicit playbook-absent and structured-output hints, ignored-artifact force-add guidance, and worktree scope classification; by teaching evidence/artifact follow-up prompts to stay in docs-first guidance instead of falling through to `create-atom`; and by aligning stale frozen-runner remediation with the real retained release command (`ATM_RETAIN_RELEASE_ARTIFACTS=1 npm run build`) so shipped runner guidance no longer points to a no-op sync path for onefile refresh. Regression coverage now lives in `scripts/validate-prompt-scoped-next.ts` and `scripts/validate-guide.ts`.
followUp: Keep prompt-scoped next hints and evidence-follow-up routing covered in the existing validators, and preserve the retained runner-sync command in runner-mode output so future guidance changes cannot quietly drift back to stale onefile behavior.
